steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure the blank requires
steps.content: The blank requires a subject and verb to form a predicate nominative after the colon: [SUBJECT + VERB] + “an island or a group of islands.” The answer choices pair two different subjects (“all of them” vs. “each of them”) with two different verb forms (“is” vs. “are”).

steps.title: Apply subject-verb agreement
steps.content:
Subject-verb agreement requires that a subject and verb match in number. “Each of them” is singular and correctly pairs with the singular verb “is,” expressing that each entity individually consists of an island or a group of islands.

The other options either mismatch subject-verb numbers (B and D) or use “all of them” where the singular distributive “each of them” is required.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
